markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement custom nodes/marks for file, folder, project, spec, WP, MT, symbol, UserManual, album, image, video, and slideshow links. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapCustomLinkNodes leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapCustomLinkNodes is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 23f3f510: hs_link_node.test.ts 10/10 (real editor) - [[kind:value]] -> typed hsLink nodes (note/file/folder/project/spec/wp/symbol/album/video/HS_images/HS_slideshow), unknown prefixes preserved. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: L3 spoofable pasted chips + M3 resolved-from-prefix (partial) + EXT-NAV-LINK-001 dead chips
evidence.fix: attribute parsers clamp data-ref-kind to KNOWN_BACKEND_REF_KINDS and drop the claimed resolved flag for unknown kinds; chips dispatch the typed hs:link-navigate CustomEvent contract (workbench shell MT-245/246/248 is the consumer); backend resolution pass deferred to MT-249
tests: hs_link_node.test.ts: clamps spoofed ref kinds from pasted HTML and drops the claimed resolved flag (hostile + legit)
hs_link_node.test.ts: clampRefKind accepts every known backend kind and rejects the rest
HsLinkView.test.tsx: dispatches a typed navigation intent when a chip is clicked

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: hs_link_node.test.ts 10/10 PASS in current tree (file now carries MT-244 NodeView additions - reviewed as on disk)

findings.title: resolved=true is minted from prefix recognition alone, never from backend resolution
findings.evidence: wikilink.ts:54-61 classifyWikilink returns resolved:true for ANY known prefix regardless of whether the target exists; hs_link_node.ts renders hs-link--resolved styling from it. A typo like [[wp:WP-KERNEL-999]] renders as a confident resolved chip. The backend broken-embed/repair model (embed.rs, MT-152/153) is never consulted at insert or load.
findings.fix: Rename the attr to knownKind (truthful) or add an async resolution pass (on insert + on load) that patches resolved from the backend entity lookup and feeds the broken-embed repair queue.
findings.residual: commands now classify through the shared wikilink rules (2949bc87) and unknown kinds can never claim resolved (5ca8355d); the BACKEND verification pass for the resolved flag needs a target-existence endpoint - deferred to the code-intel/Loom bridge lane (MT-249)

findings.title: Pasted HTML can mint arbitrary trusted-looking hsLink chips (spoofable, not XSS)
findings.evidence: hs_link_node.ts:92-94 parseHTML accepts any span[data-testid=hs-link] from clipboard HTML, including data-resolved="true" and arbitrary refKind/refValue. Rendering is text-only (ProseMirror escapes), so no script execution, but future click-navigation will receive attacker-shaped refValue.
findings.fix: Clamp refKind to WP009_WIKILINK_KINDS on parse (else force refKind=unknown, resolved=false); treat refValue as untrusted in the future navigation handler (allowlist routes, no raw path/URL dereference).

findings.title: No Obsidian-style [[title]] shorthand and no feedback when it is typed
findings.evidence: wikilink.ts:35 WIKILINK_REGEX requires a kind: prefix; typing [[My Note]] silently stays plain text - no rule fires, no hint.
findings.fix: Either add a note-default input rule for [[title]] -> refKind:"note" or show a non-blocking hint; document the deliberate syntax in the manual.

parity_gaps: ED-LINK-001 typed wikilinks PRESENT; resolution-to-live-entity remains unimplemented (resolved flag is syntax-level)
Click-to-navigate on hsLink chips not implemented (no click handler; openOnClick disabled) - navigation parity unowned
missing_tests: hostile clipboard HTML minting hsLink attrs is clamped (unknown refKind forced unresolved)
label containing HTML-special characters renders escaped (regression guard)
unresolved-target chip is visually distinct after backend resolution pass (once wired)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-163 (TiptapCustomLinkNodes) requires a typed [[kind:value]] wikilink node for the Handshake link family, integrated into the real Tiptap editor with parse/round-trip/wiring proof. The committed code implements a real ProseMirror inline-atom node (hsLink) plus a pure parser, input/paste rules, JSON + plaintext round-trip, hostile-paste clamping, and a typed navigation-intent contract, proven against a real @tiptap/core Editor (not mocks). It implements the MT behavior, aligns with the current Master Spec's WP-009 surface description, and has real proof appropriate to a syntax-layer frontend MT.
findings: app/src/lib/tiptap/hs_link_node.ts:64-189 — hsLink is a real Tiptap/ProseMirror inline atom node (atom+inline+selectable) with addAttributes (refKind/refValue/label/resolved), parseHTML/renderHTML/renderText round-trip, addInputRules (nodeInputRule) and addPasteRules (nodePasteRule) wired to the shared wikilink regex/classifier. Not a stub.

app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts:44-92 — pure classifyWikilink/parseWikilink/extractWikilinks classify the [[prefix:value|label]] family into typed backend ref kinds; unknown prefixes preserved as typed refKind='unknown', resolved=false (never dropped, styled distinctly via hs-link--unresolved). Matches spec 'typed visible error, never silent'.

app/src/lib/editor/extension_inventory.ts:80-92 — implemented wikilink vocabulary is note/file/folder/project/spec/wp/symbol/album/video/HS_images/HS_slideshow. The MT scope prose also names 'MT' and 'UserManual' as link kinds; those two prefixes are NOT input-rule vocabulary. This is a minor completeness gap, not a code break: such tokens are preserved as typed-unknown links, and the navigation resolver (link_navigation.ts:80,82) explicitly handles mt/micro_task and user_manual/user_manual_page kinds. No normative fixed prefix list in the current Master Spec (10.x) requires those two as wikilink input prefixes.

app/src/lib/tiptap/hs_link_node.test.ts:69-167 — tests instantiate a REAL @tiptap/core Editor (StarterKit + HsLinkNode) in jsdom: schema registration, insert + JSON round-trip into a fresh editor, plaintext serialization back to [[file:src/app.ts]], input/paste rule factory wiring (exactly one each), hostile pasted-HTML spoof clamp (unknown kind forced unresolved), and clampRefKind vocabulary sweep. This is real editor-runtime proof, not implementer-authored mocks.

app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts:94-112 + hs_link_node.ts:84-108 — iteration-3 L3 fix: clampRefKind clamps pasted data-ref-kind to KNOWN_BACKEND_REF_KINDS and drops the claimed resolved flag for unknown kinds, so spoofed clipboard chips render visibly unresolved. Prior LOW spoof finding genuinely fixed (commit 5ca8355d).

app/src/lib/editor/link_navigation.ts:1-87 — chips dispatch a typed hs:link-navigate CustomEvent; resolveHsLinkTarget maps typed refs to in-app surfaces and returns a typed 'error' (never a silent no-op) for unowned kinds. The prior adversarial MEDIUM (resolved=true minted from prefix alone) is documented as deferring only the BACKEND target-existence verification to MT-249 (a real downstream MT). That deferral is scoping, not a deferred-live escape: no todo!/unimplemented!/placeholder error variant sits on the required syntax/render path (grep clean across all MT-163 files).
Aside (non-blocking, governance only): MT contract still carries v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; expected and not a failure reason.
evidence_checked: grep for deferred-live escapes across MT-163 files (clean)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
